name: Peter Mennin
abstract: Peter Mennin (born Mennini; May 17, 1923 – June 17, 1983) was a prominent American composer, teacher and administrator. In 1958, he was named Director of the Peabody Conservatory in Baltimore, and in 1962 became President of the Juilliard School, a position he held until his death in 1983.

about_quotes.speaker: Tom McIntosh
about_quotes.evidence: I was put in a class with composers and all of them were subjected to this teacher who was a composer himself, brilliant, Peter Mennin.
about_quotes.doc_title: Tom McIntosh — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Dan Morgenstern
about_quotes.evidence: Memorably, Peter Mennin, who was the head of the music panel, said, “Jazz. That’s a music played in nightclubs.”
about_quotes.doc_title: Dan Morgenstern — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
